# agogo

> A compact, stdlib-first web server in pure Go. It renders HTML (for SEO) and a
> JSON API from a single source of truth, with no web framework — just the
> standard library (`net/http`, `html/template`, `encoding/json`) and a few niche
> tools. This repository is a clean starter you grow by adding modules.

Key facts for an agent working here:

- **Runtime:** Go 1.26+. No build step; scc (CSS) and lumen (JS) load from the
  jsDelivr CDN (lumen via an inline import map; `scripts/vendor-frontend.sh`
  self-hosts both for a strict-`'self'` CSP). The only dependency is
  `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure Go, no cgo → static binary).
- **Shape:** a host (`app`) you plug opt-in modules into with `app.Use(...)` in
  `main.go`. A module implements `Module{ Name() string; Register(*App) error }`
  and hooks into the host (routes, middleware, migrations, sitemap, services).
  What you don't wire in never enters the binary.
- **Layout:** the framework toolbox lives in `internal/` (core packages, not
  modules); sections are modules (`internal/home`, `internal/site`, …).
- **Philosophy:** only the essentials, no magic (no ORM, no reflection config),
  minimal core + pluggable modules, YAGNI first / DRY later. The code that runs
  is the code you read — it all lives in `internal/`.
- **Conventions:** identifiers (variables, functions, types) in English; the
  documentation site and user-facing strings are bilingual ES/EN; the technical
  API reference is English-only.

## Documentation

- [Documentation site](https://dragones-tech.github.io/agogo/): bilingual ES/EN guide (getting started, creating a module, structure, activating modules, over-the-wire) plus a tree-navigated technical API reference generated with go/doc.
- [README](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/blob/main/README.md): quickstart, philosophy, configuration, structure, security, deploy.
- [ARCHITECTURE](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md): design notes and patterns.
- [main.go](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/blob/main/main.go): the wiring — creates the App and plugs modules with `app.Use(...)`. The clearest overview of what the server does.

## Core toolbox (internal/)

- [app](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/app): the host — App, the Module interface, and the hook points modules register into.
- [router](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/router): an Express-style router on top of `net/http`, with middleware support.
- [config](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/config): typed environment config, including the SQLite DSN (WAL + `busy_timeout`).
- [middleware](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/middleware): baseline middlewares — Recover, LimitBody, SecurityHeaders (CSP), Gzip.
- [view](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/view): shared HTML layout and Render (renders to a buffer; supports X-Fragment partial navigation).
- [respond](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/respond) and [validate](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/validate): JSON responses and a tiny input validator.
- [web](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/web): generic `Resource[T]` that writes the list/detail × HTML/JSON handlers, route registration and sitemap contribution once.
- [session](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/session), [identity](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/identity), [password](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/password), [csrf](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/csrf): the auth/session toolbox — signed-cookie sessions (HMAC), login/logout, PBKDF2 hashing, double-cookie CSRF.
- [jsonld](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/jsonld) and [sitemap](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/sitemap): schema.org JSON-LD and sitemap generation.

## Modules (sections)

- [home](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/home): the active "hello world" section — the smallest module, a good template to copy.
- [otw](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/otw): active "HTML over the wire" BFF — calls a token-gated external API server-side and returns a rendered HTML fragment (the token never reaches the client). Works without credentials via a built-in simulated endpoint; its demo button lives on the `/ejemplo` page.
- [paginas](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/paginas): active example static section at `/ejemplo` (also hosts the otw demo button) — the simplest domain shape (no DB); copy it for your own sections.
- [auth](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/auth): opt-in username/password login (`/login`, `/cuenta`) — PBKDF2, CSRF, reuses `identity`. Needs the DB: run `cmd/migrate` (and `cmd/seed`) before enabling it in `main.go`.
- [oauth](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/oauth): opt-in OAuth 2.0 login (authorization-code flow), pure stdlib, reuses `identity`. No DB; routes answer 503 until `OAUTH_*` env is set.
- [site](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/site): robots.txt, sitemap.xml, `/static`, favicon and the styled 404.
- [logs](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/internal/logs): opt-in access-logging middleware.

The dev commands [cmd/migrate](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/cmd/migrate) and [cmd/seed](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/tree/main/cmd/seed) live outside the server: each builds the same App and runs `app.Migrate(ctx)` to apply a DB module's schema / seed demo data.

## Optional

- [Dockerfile](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/blob/main/Dockerfile): multi-stage build to a `scratch` image, non-root user.
- [sqlc.yaml](https://github.com/dragones-tech/agogo/blob/main/sqlc.yaml): sqlc config — one typed function per SQL query, no ORM.
